skill: One-variable data: Distributions and measures of center and spread
rationale:
Choice A is correct. The median of a data set with an odd number of values that are in ascending or descending order is the middle value of the data set. Since the distribution of the values of both data set A and data set B form symmetric dot plots, and each data set has an odd number of values, it follows that the median is given by the middle value in each of the dot plots. Thus, the median of data set A is $13$, and the median of data set B is $13$. Therefore, statement I is true. Data set A and data set B have the same frequency for each of the values $11$, $12$, $14$, and $15$. Data set A has a frequency of $1$ for values $10$ and $16$, whereas data set B has a frequency of $2$ for values $10$ and $16$. Standard deviation is a measure of the spread of a data set; it is larger when there are more values further from the mean, and smaller when there are more values closer to the mean. Since both distributions are symmetric with an odd number of values, the mean of each data set is equal to its median. Thus, each data set has a mean of $13$. Since more of the values in data set A are closer to $13$ than data set B, it follows that data set A has a smaller standard deviation than data set B. Thus, statement II is false. Therefore, only statement I must be true.
Choice B is incorrect and may result from conceptual or calculation errors.
Choice C is incorrect and may result from conceptual or calculation errors.
Choice D is incorrect and may result from conceptual or calculation errors.

skill: Ratios, rates, proportional relationships, and units
rationale:
Choice B is correct. It's given that the speed of a vehicle is increasing at a rate of $10.7$ meters per second squared. It's given to use $1\text{mile}=1,609\text{meters}$. There are $60$ seconds in $1$ minute; therefore, ${60}^{2}$ or $3,600$ seconds squared is equal to $1$ minute squared. It follows that the rate of $10.7$ meters per second squared is equivalent to $\left(\frac{10.7\text{meters}}{1\text{second squared}}\right) \left(\frac{1\text{mile}}{1,609\text{meters}}\right) \left(\frac{3,600\text{seconds squared}}{1\text{minute squared}}\right)$, or approximately $23.94\text{miles per minute squared}$. The rate, $\text{in miles per minute squared}$, rounded to the nearest tenth is $23.9$.
Choice A is incorrect and may result from conceptual or calculation errors.
Choice C is incorrect and may result from conceptual or calculation errors.
Choice D is incorrect and may result from conceptual or calculation errors.

skill: Ratios, rates, proportional relationships, and units
rationale: Choice A is correct. It's given that rectangle A has length 12 and width w. Therefore, the length-to-width ratio of rectangle A is 12 to w. It's also given that rectangle B has length 18 and the same length-to-width ratio as rectangle A. Let x represent the width of rectangle B. The proportion $12 / w = 18 / x$ can be used to solve for x in terms of w. Multiplying both sides of this equation by x yields $\frac{12x}{w} = 18$, and then multiplying both sides of this equation by w yields $12x = 18w$. Dividing both sides of this equation by 12 yields $x = \frac{18w}{12}$. Simplifying this fraction yields $x = \frac{3}{2} w$. Choices B and D are incorrect and may result from interpreting the difference in the lengths of rectangle A and rectangle B as equivalent to the difference in the widths of rectangle A and rectangle B. Choice C is incorrect and may result from using a length-to-width ratio of w to 12, instead of 12 to w.

rationale:
Choice B is correct. The mean of a data set is the sum of the values in the data set divided by the number of values in the data set. The new data set consists of the weights of the $71$ tortoises in the original data set and one additional weight, $39$. Since the additional weight, $39$, is greater than any of the values in the original data set, the mean of the new data set is greater than the mean of the original data set. If a data set contains an odd number of data values, the median is represented by the middle data value in the list when the data values are listed in ascending or descending order. Since the original data set consists of the weights of $71$ tortoises and is in ascending order, the median of the original data set is represented by the middle value, or the $36\text{th}$ value. Based on the frequencies shown in the table, the $36\text{th}$ value in this data set is $17$. If a data set contains an even number of data values, the median is between the two middle data values when the values are listed in ascending or descending order. Since the new data set consists of the weights of $72$ tortoises, the median of the new data set is between the $36\text{th}$ and $37\text{th}$ data values when the values are arranged in ascending order. To keep the data in ascending order, the additional value of $39$ would be placed at the bottom of the frequency table with a frequency of $1$. Therefore, based on the frequencies in the table, the $36\text{th}$ and $37\text{th}$ values in the new data set are both $17$. It follows that the median of the new data set is $17$, which is the same as the median of the original data set. Therefore, the mean of the new data set is greater than the mean of the original data set, and the medians of the two data sets are equal.
Choice A is incorrect and may result from conceptual or calculation errors.
Choice C is incorrect and may result from conceptual or calculation errors.
Choice D is incorrect and may result from conceptual or calculation errors.

rationale:
Choice A is correct. The median is the middle value in a data set when the data are arranged in order from least to greatest. Since there are $15$ values in data set A, the median is the $8\text{th}$ value. The $8\text{th}$ value is $23$, so the median of data set A is $23$. The range is found by subtracting the minimum value in a data set from the maximum value. The minimum value in data set A is $22$ and the maximum value is $26$. Therefore, the range of data set A is $26-22$, or $4$. It's given that data set B is created by adding $46$ to each of the values in data set A. Therefore, the $8\text{th}$ value in data set B is $23+46$, or $69$, so the median of data set B is $69$. The minimum value in data set B is $22+46$, or $68$, and the maximum value is $26+46$, or $72$. Therefore, the range of data set B is $72-68$, or $4$. Since the median of data set A is $23$, and the median of data set B is $69$, the median of data set B is greater than the median of data set A. Since the ranges of data sets A and B are both $4$, the range of data set B is equal to the range of data set A.
Choice B is incorrect and may result from conceptual or calculation errors.
Choice C is incorrect and may result from conceptual or calculation errors.
Choice D is incorrect and may result from conceptual or calculation errors.

Since the next $7$ restaurants each have $20$ to $25$ employees, numbers of employees in the $20$ to $25$ range will be represented by the ninth through fifteenth restaurants in the list. rationale:
Choice B is correct. The histograms shown have the same shape, but data set A contains values between $20$ and $60$ and data set B contains values between $10$ and $50$. Thus, the mean of data set A is greater than the mean of data set B. Therefore, the smallest possible difference between the mean of data set A and the mean of data set B is the difference between the smallest possible mean of data set A and the greatest possible mean of data set B. In data set A, since there are $3$ integers in the interval greater than or equal to $20$ but less than $30$, $4$ integers greater than or equal to $30$ but less than $40$, $7$ integers greater than or equal to $40$ but less than $50$, and $9$ integers greater than or equal to $50$ but less than $60$, the smallest possible mean for data set A is $\frac{ \left(3·20\right)+ \left(4·30\right)+ \left(7·40\right)+ \left(9·50\right)}{23}$. In data set B, since there are $3$ integers greater than or equal to $10$ but less than $20$, $4$ integers greater than or equal to $20$ but less than $30$, $7$ integers greater than or equal to $30$ but less than $40$, and $9$ integers greater than or equal to $40$ but less than $50$, the largest possible mean for data set B is $\frac{ \left(3·19\right)+ \left(4·29\right)+ \left(7·39\right)+ \left(9·49\right)}{23}$. Therefore, the smallest possible difference between the mean of data set A and the mean of data set B is $\frac{ \left(3·20\right)+ \left(4·30\right)+ \left(7·40\right)+ \left(9·50\right)}{23}-\frac{ \left(3·19\right)+ \left(4·29\right)+ \left(7·39\right)+ \left(9·49\right)}{23}$, which is equivalent to $\frac{ \left(3·20\right)- \left(3·19\right)+ \left(4·30\right)- \left(4·29\right)+ \left(7·40\right)- \left(7·39\right)+ \left(9·50\right)- \left(9·49\right)}{23}$. This expression can be rewritten as $\frac{3 \left(20-19\right)+4 \left(30-29\right)+7 \left(40-39\right)+9 \left(50-49\right)}{23}$, or $\frac{23}{23}$, which is equal to $1$. Therefore, the smallest possible difference between the mean of data set A and the mean of data set B is $1$.
Choice A is incorrect. This is the smallest possible difference between the ranges, not the means, of the data sets.
Choice C is incorrect. This is the difference between the greatest possible mean, not the smallest possible mean, of data set A and the greatest possible mean of data set B.
Choice D is incorrect. This is the smallest possible difference between the sum of the values in data set A and the sum of the values in data set B, not the smallest possible difference between the means.

rationale: The correct answer is $\frac{1}{13}$. If one vertex of the polygon is selected at random, the probability that the letter $H$ will be at the selected vertex is equal to the number of vertices labeled with the letter $H$ divided by the total number of vertices. It's given that each vertex is labeled with one of the $13$ letters $A$ through $M$, with a different letter at each vertex. It follows that there is $1$ vertex labeled with the letter $H$. It's also given that the polygon is $13$-sided. It follows that there are a total of $13$ vertices. Thus, the probability that the letter $H$ will be at the selected vertex is $\frac{1}{13}$. Note that 1/13,.0769, and 0.077 are examples of ways to enter a correct answer.

rationale:
Choice C is correct. If the mean score of 8 players is 16.5, then the total of all 8 scores is $16.5 \times 8 = 132$. If the mean of 7 scores is 14, then the total of all 7 scores is $14 \times 7 = 98$. Since the set of 7 scores was made by removing the highest score of the set of 8 scores, then the difference between the total of all 8 scores and the total of all 7 scores is equal to the removed score: $132 - 98 = 34$.
Choice A is incorrect because if 22 is removed from the group of 8 scores, then the mean score of the remaining 7 players is $\frac{(16.5 \times 8) - 22}{7} \approx 15.71$, not 14. Choice B is incorrect because if 26 is removed from the group of 8 scores, then the mean score of the remaining 7 players is $\frac{(16.5 \times 8) - 26}{7} \approx 15.14$, not 14. Choice D is incorrect because if 38 is removed from the group of 8 scores, then the mean score of the remaining 7 players is $\frac{(16.5 \times 8) - 38}{7} \approx 13.43$, not 14.

rationale:
Choice B is correct. It's given that, based on the sample, an estimate of $14%$ of all gloves produced by the company in a certain week are defective, with an associated margin of error of $2.75%$. This estimate, plus or minus the margin of error, gives an interval of plausible values for the actual percent of all gloves produced by the company that week that are defective. Subtracting $2.75%$ from $14%$ yields $11.25%$. Adding $2.75%$ to $14%$ yields $16.75%$. Therefore, it is plausible that between $11.25%$ and $16.75%$ of all gloves produced by the company are defective.
Choice A is incorrect and may result from conceptual errors.
Choice C is incorrect. $14%$ is the estimated percent of defective gloves based on the sample. However, since the margin of error for this estimate is known, the most appropriate conclusion is not that the percent of defective gloves is exactly $14%$ but instead that it lies in an interval of plausible percents.
Choice D is incorrect and may result from conceptual errors.

rationale: Choice D is correct. On the line of best fit, d increases from approximately 480 to 880 between $t = 12$ and $t = 24$. The slope of the line of best fit is the difference in d-values divided by the difference in t-values, which gives $\frac{880 - 480}{24 - 12} = \frac{400}{12} \approx 33$. Writing the equation of the line of best fit in slope-intercept form gives $d = 33t + b$, where b is the y-coordinate of the y-intercept. This equation is satisfied by all points on the line, so $d = 480$ when $t = 12$. Thus, $480 = 33 \times 12 + b$, which is equivalent to $480 = 396 + b$. Subtracting 396 from both sides of this equation gives $b = 84$. Therefore, an equation for the line of best fit could be $d = 33t + 84$. Choice A is incorrect and may result from an error in calculating the slope and misidentifying the y-coordinate of the y-intercept of the graph as the value of d at rather than the value of d at $t = 0$. Choice B is incorrect and may result from using the smallest value of t on the graph as the slope and misidentifying the y-coordinate of the y-intercept of the graph as the value of d at $t = 10$ rather than the value of d at $t = 0$. Choice C is incorrect and may result from misidentifying the y-coordinate of the y-intercept as the smallest value of d on the graph.

rationale:
Choice C is correct. It's given that the olive sorting machine sorts $18$ pounds of olives in $6$ minutes. This rate can be written as $\frac{18\text{pounds}\text{of}\text{olives}}{6\text{minutes}}$. If the number of minutes it takes the machine to sort $108$ pounds of olives is represented by $x$, the value of $x$ can be calculated by solving the equation $\frac{18\text{pounds}\text{of}\text{olives}}{6\text{minutes}}=\frac{108\text{pounds}\text{of}\text{olives}}{x\text{minutes}}$, which can be rewritten as $\frac{18}{6}=\frac{108}{x}$, or $3=\frac{108}{x}$. Multiplying each side of this equation by $x$ yields $3x=108$. Dividing each side of this equation by $3$ yields $x=36$. Therefore, it takes the machine $36$ minutes to sort $108$ pounds of olives.
Choice A is incorrect. This is the number of $18$-pound batches in $108$ pounds of olives, not the number of minutes it takes to sort $108$ pounds of olives.
Choice B is incorrect. This is the number of minutes it takes the machine to sort $72$, not $108$, pounds of olives.
Choice D is incorrect. This is the number of minutes it takes the machine to sort $324$, not $108$, pounds of olives.

Choice B is correct. It was estimated that 20% of the beads in the bag are blue. Since the bag contains 12,000 beads, it follows that there are an estimated $12 000 \times 0.20 = 2 400$ blue beads. It's given that the margin of error is 3%, or $12 000 \times 0.03 = 360$ beads. If the estimate is too high, there could plausibly be $2 400 - 360 = 2 040$ blue beads. If the estimate is too low, there could plausibly be $2 400 + 360 = 2 760$ blue beads. Therefore, the most plausible statement of the actual number of blue beads in the bag is 2,040 is less than r, which is less than 2,760.
Choices A and D are incorrect and may result from misinterpreting the margin of error. It's unlikely that more than 2,760 beads or fewer than 2,040 beads in the bag are blue. skill: One-variable data: Distributions and measures of center and spread
rationale:
Choice C is correct. It's given that the dot plot represents a data set of the number of bursts for $13$ eruptions of a steam vent. The median of a data set with an odd number of elements is the middle element when the elements are in numerical order. For $13$ elements in numerical order, this is the $7\text{th}$ element. For this data set, the first $4$ elements have a value of $1$, and the next $5$ elements have a value of $2$. Thus, the $7\text{th}$ element in the ordered data set is $2$ and the median number of bursts for the original data set is $2$. If an additional eruption with $11$ bursts is added to this data set to create a new data set of $14$ eruptions, the median of the new data set will be between the $7\text{th}$ and $8\text{th}$ elements in the ordered set, which will also be $2$. Therefore, the median number of bursts for the new data set will be the same as the median number of bursts for the original data set. The mean number of bursts for the original data set is found by adding the values of all $13$ elements and dividing that sum by the number of elements, $13$. Since the data is shown in a dot plot, the sum of the values of the elements can be found by multiplying each element's value by its frequency: $1 \left(4\right)+2 \left(5\right)+3 \left(2\right)+4 \left(1\right)+5 \left(1\right)$, or $29$. Therefore, the mean number of bursts for the original data set is $\frac{29}{13}$. If an additional eruption with $11$ bursts is added to this data set to create a new data set of $14$ bursts, the mean number of bursts for the new data set is $\frac{29+11}{14}$, or $\frac{40}{14}$. Since $\frac{40}{14} \gt \frac{29}{13}$, the mean number of bursts for the new data set is greater than the mean number of bursts for the original data set. Therefore, of the median number of bursts and the mean number of bursts, only the mean number of bursts is greater for the new data set than for the original data set.
Choice A is incorrect and may result from conceptual or calculation errors.
Choice B is incorrect and may result from conceptual or calculation errors.
Choice D is incorrect and may result from conceptual or calculation errors.

rationale: The correct answer is $.05$. It's given that $\frac{5a}{b}=8.25$ and $\frac{a}{bn}=33$. The equation $\frac{5a}{b}=8.25$ can be rewritten as $\left(5\right) \left(\frac{a}{b}\right)=8.25$. Dividing both sides of this equation by $5$ yields $\frac{a}{b}=1.65$. The equation $\frac{a}{bn}=33$ can be rewritten as $\left(\frac{a}{b}\right) \left(\frac{1}{n}\right)=33$. Substituting $1.65$ for $\frac{a}{b}$ in this equation yields $\left(1.65\right) \left(\frac{1}{n}\right)=33$, or $\frac{1.65}{n}=33$. Multiplying both sides of this equation by $n$ yields $1.65=33n$. Dividing both sides of this equation by $33$ yields $n=0.05$. Therefore, the value of $n$ is $0.05$. Note that .05, 0.05, and 1/20 are examples of ways to enter a correct answer.

It's also given that k is the product of 75 and r, which can be rewritten as $k = 75r$. Substituting 93 for k yields $93 = 75r$. Dividing both sides of this equation by 75 yields $r = 1.24$. rationale:
Choice C is correct. The weight of an object on Planet R is approximately $\frac{4}{5}$ of its weight on Earth. If an object weighs 200 pounds on Earth, then the object's weight on Planet R is approximately $\frac{4}{5} \times 200 = 160$ pounds. The same object's weight on Planet S is approximately $\frac{9}{5}$ of its weight on Earth; therefore, the object weighs approximately $\frac{9}{5} \times 200 = 360$ pounds on Planet S. The difference between the object's weight on Planet S and the object's weight on Planet R is approximately $360 - 160 = 200$ pounds. Therefore, an object that weighs 200 pounds on Earth weighs 200 more pounds on Planet S than it weighs on Planet R.
Choice A is incorrect because it is the weight, in pounds, of the object on Planet R. Choice B is incorrect because it is the weight, in pounds, of an object on Earth if it weighs 200 pounds on Planet R. Choice D is incorrect because it is the weight, in pounds, of the object on Planet S.

rationale:
Choice C is correct. If the values in a data set are ordered from least to greatest, the median of the data set will be the middle value. Since each data set in the choices is ordered and contains exactly 9 data values, the 5th value in each is the median. It follows that the median of the data set in choice C is 48. The sum of the positive differences between 48 and each of the values that are less than 48 is significantly smaller than the sum of the positive differences between 48 and each of the values that are greater than 48. If 48 were the mean, these sums would have been equal to each other. Therefore, the mean of this data set must be greater than 48. This can also be confirmed by calculating the mean as the sum of the values divided by the number of values in the data set: $\frac{3 + 6 + 12 + 24 + 48 + 96 + 192 + 384 + 768}{9} = 170\frac{1}{3}$.
Choices A and B are incorrect. Each of the data sets in these choices is symmetric with respect to its median, so the mean and the median for each of these choices are equivalent. Choice D is incorrect. The median of this data set is 209. Since the sum of the positive differences between 209 and each of the values less than 209 is greater than the sum of the positive differences between 209 and each value greater than 209 in this data set, the mean must be less than the median.

An equation for the line can be written in slope-intercept form, $y = m x + b$, where m is the slope and b is the y-coordinate of the y-intercept. The equation $y = 3.30x + 0.82$ in choice C fits the data most closely. skill: Inference from sample statistics and margin of error
rationale:
Choice D is correct. Sample size is an appropriate reason for the margin of error to change. In general, a smaller sample size increases the margin of error because the sample may be less representative of the whole population.
Choice A is incorrect. The margin of error will depend on the size of the sample of recorded votes, not the number of votes that could not be recorded. In any case, the smaller number of votes that could not be recorded for sample A would tend to decrease, not increase, the comparative size of the margin of error. Choice B is incorrect. Since the percent in favor for sample A is the same distance from 50% as the percent in favor for sample B, the percent of favorable responses doesn't affect the comparative size of the margin of error for the two samples. Choice C is incorrect. If sample A had a larger margin of error than sample B, then sample A would tend to be less representative of the population. Therefore, sample A is not likely to have a larger sample size.

rationale:
Choice D is correct. It's given that in $2013$ Maya earned $12%$ more than in $2012$. Let $h$ represent the amount Maya earned in $2012$ and let $j$ represent the amount that Maya earned in $2013$. This situation can be represented by the equation $j= \left(1+\frac{12}{100}\right)h$, or $j=1.12h$. It's also given that in $2014$ Maya earned $5%$ more than in $2013$. Let $k$ represent the amount Maya earned in $2014$. This situation can be represented by the equation $k= \left(1+\frac{5}{100}\right)j$, or $k=1.05j$. Substituting $1.12h$ for $j$ in the equation $k=1.05j$ yields $k= \left(1.05\right) \left(1.12h\right)$, or $k=1.1760h$. If Maya earned $y$ times as much in $2014$ as in $2012$, then the value of $y$ is $1.1760$.
Choice A is incorrect and may result from treating the product of 12 and 5 as the final multiplier. Choice B is incorrect and may result from including only the product of the percentage rates. Choice C is incorrect and may result from adding the percentage increases without accounting for compounding.

rationale: The correct answer is $1,023$. It's given that the first moon orbits the planet every $236$ days. Therefore, it takes the first moon $236 \left(31\right)$, or $7,316$, days to orbit the planet $31$ times. It's also given that the second moon orbits the planet every $269$ days. Therefore, it takes the second moon $269 \left(31\right)$, or $8,339$, days to orbit the planet $31$ times. Since it takes the first moon $7,316$ days and the second moon $8,339$ days, it takes the second moon $8,339-7,316$, or $1,023$, more days than it takes the first moon to orbit the planet $31$ times.

rationale: Choice B is correct. It's given that the density of a certain type of plastic is $347$ kilograms per cubic meter $\left({\text{kg/m}}^{3}\right)$, and a sample of this type of plastic has a mass of $358\text{kg}$. Let $x$ represent the volume, in ${\text{m}}^{3}$, of the sample. It follows that the relationship between the density, mass, and volume of this sample can be written as $\frac{347\text{kg}}{1{\text{m}}^{3}}=\frac{358\text{kg}}{x{\text{m}}^{3}}$, or $347=\frac{358}{x}$. Multiplying both sides of this equation by $x$ yields $347x=358$. Dividing both sides of this equation by $347$ yields $x=\frac{358}{347}$. Therefore, the volume of this sample is $\frac{358}{347}{\text{m}}^{3}$. Since it's given that the sample of this type of plastic is a cube, it follows that the length of one edge of this sample can be found using the volume formula for a cube, $V={s}^{3}$, where $V$ represents the volume, in ${\text{m}}^{3}$, and $s$ represents the length, in m, of one edge of the cube. Substituting $\frac{358}{347}$ for $V$ in this formula yields $\frac{358}{347}={s}^{3}$. Taking the cube root of both sides of this equation yields $\sqrt[3]{\frac{358}{347}}=s$, or $s\approx1.01$. Therefore, the length of one edge of this sample to the nearest hundredth of a meter is $1.01$. Choices A, C, and D are incorrect and may result from conceptual or calculation errors.

rationale: The correct answer is $.56$. It's given that the number $a$ is $65%$ less than the positive number $b$. Therefore, $a= \left(1-\frac{65}{100}\right)b$, which is equivalent to $a= \left(1-0.65\right)b$, or $a=0.35b$. It's also given that the number $c$ is $60%$ greater than $a$. Therefore, $c= \left(1+\frac{60}{100}\right)a$, which is equivalent to $c= \left(1+0.60\right)a$, or $c=1.60a$. Since $a=0.35b$, substituting $0.35b$ for $a$ in the equation $c=1.60a$ yields $c=1.60 \left(0.35b\right)$, or $c=0.56b$. Thus, $c$ is $0.56$ times $b$. Note that .56 and 14/25 are examples of ways to enter a correct answer.

rationale:
Choice C is correct. It's given that an estimated $40%$ of people in the population support the ordinance, with an associated margin of error of $4%$. Subtracting and adding the margin of error from the estimate gives an interval of plausible values for the true percentage of people in the population who support the ordinance. Therefore, it's plausible that between $36%$ and $44%$ of people in this population support the ordinance. The corresponding numbers of people represented by these percentages in the population can be calculated by multiplying the total population, $60,000$, by $0.36$ and by $0.44$, which gives $60,000 \left(0.36\right)=21,600$ and $60,000 \left(0.44\right)=26,400$, respectively. It follows that any value in the interval $21,600$ to $26,400$ is a plausible value for the total number of people in the population who support the proposed ordinance. Of the choices given, only $24,600$ is in this interval.
Choice A is incorrect. This is the number of people in the sample, rather than in the population, who support the ordinance.
Choice B is incorrect. This is the number of people in the sample who do not support the ordinance.
Choice D is incorrect. This is a plausible value for the total number of people in the population who do not support the proposed ordinance.

If a data set contains an even number of data values, the median is between the two middle data values when the values are listed in ascending or descending order. Since each of the choices consists of a data set with $6$ data values in ascending order, it follows that the median is between the third and fourth data value. The third and fourth data values in choice B are $9$ and $9$. Thus, choice B represents a data set with a median of $9$. Since the median of the data set shown is $9$ and choice B represents a data set with a median of $9$, it follows that choice B represents a data set that has the same median as the data set shown.
Choice A is incorrect. This list represents a data set with a median of $7$, not $9$.

rationale:
Choice D is correct. The members of the town council wanted to assess opinions of all town residents. To gather an unbiased sample, the council should have used a random sampling design to select subjects from all town residents. The given survey introduced a sampling bias because the 600 town residents surveyed were all skateboard owners. This sample is not representative of all town residents because not all town residents own skateboards.
Choice A is incorrect because when the sampling method isn't random, there is no guarantee that the survey results will be reliable; hence, they cannot be generalized to the entire population. Choice B is incorrect because a larger sample of residents who own skateboards would not correct the sampling bias. Choice C is incorrect because a survey sample of entirely non-skateboard owners would likely have a biased opinion, just as a sample of skateboard owners would likely have a biased opinion.

Therefore, the mean of data set A is greater than the mean of data set B.
Alternate approach: Data set A and data set B are both ordered from least to greatest value. Besides the first value in each data set, which is 1, each value in ordered data set B is less than the respective value in ordered data set A. rationale:
Choice D is correct. The change in the number of 3-D movies released between any two consecutive years can be found by first estimating the number of 3-D movies released for each of the two years and then finding the positive difference between these two estimates. Between 2003 and 2004, this change is approximately $2 - 2 = 0$ movies; between 2008 and 2009, this change is approximately $20 - 8 = 12$ movies; between 2009 and 2010, this change is approximately $26 - 20 = 6$ movies; and between 2010 and 2011, this change is approximately $46 - 26 = 20$ movies. Therefore, of the pairs of consecutive years in the choices, the greatest increase in the number of 3-D movies released occurred during the time period between 2010 and 2011.
Choices A, B, and C are incorrect. Between 2010 and 2011, approximately 20 more 3-D movies were released. The change in the number of 3-D movies released between any of the other pairs of consecutive years is significantly smaller than 20.
text: According to the line graph above, between which two consecutive years was there the greatest change in the number of 3‑D movies released?

rationale: The correct answer is $62,500$. It's given that the weight of sample A is $375\%$ of the weight of sample B and $0.600\%$ of the weight of sample C. Let the weight of sample B be $b$ and the weight of sample C be $c$. Then, the weight of sample A is equivalent to $\frac{375b}{100}$, or $3.75b$, and also equivalent to $\frac{0.600c}{100}$, or $0.00600c$. Setting these expressions equal to each other yields $3.75b=0.00600c$. Dividing both sides of this equation by $0.00600$ yields $\frac{3.75b}{0.00600}=c$, or $c=625b$. If the weight of sample C is $p\%$ of the weight of sample B, then $c=\frac{p}{100}b$. Since $c=625b$ and $c=\frac{p}{100}b$, it follows that $625b=\frac{p}{100}b$, or $625=\frac{p}{100}$. Multiplying both sides of this equation by $100$ yields $62,500=p$. Therefore, the value of $p$ is $62,500$.
text: The weight of sample A is $375\%$ of the weight of sample B, and the weight of sample A is $0.600\%$ of the weight of sample C. If the weight of sample C is $p\%$ of the weight of sample B, what is the value of $p$?

skill: Ratios, rates, proportional relationships, and units
rationale:
Choice A is correct. If 3.5 ounces of blueberries are needed for each scone, then the number of ounces of blueberries needed to make 40 scones is $40 \times 3.5 = 140$ ounces. Since 1 pound = 16 ounces, the number of pounds that is equivalent to 140 ounces is $140 / 16 = 8.75$ pounds. Therefore, 8.75 pounds of blueberries are needed to make the 40 scones.
Choice B is incorrect. This answer results from using 12 ounces per pound instead of 16 ounces per pound: $140 / 12 \approx 11.67$ pounds. Choices C and D are also incorrect. Following the same procedures as used to test choice B gives 17.4 ounces per scone for choice C and 56 ounces per scone for choice D, not 3.5 ounces per scone. Therefore, 43.5 and 140 pounds cannot be the number of pounds needed to make 40 signature blueberry scones.

skill: Evaluating statistical claims: Observational studies and experiments
rationale:
Choice D is correct. It's given that the organizer selected $55$ teams at random from all trivia teams in the tournament. A table is also given displaying the information for the $40$ teams in the sample that practiced for at least $3$ hours per week. Selecting a sample of a reasonable size at random to use for a survey allows the results from that survey to be applied to the population from which the sample was selected, but not beyond this population. Thus, only the sampling method information is necessary to determine the largest population to which the results of the study can be generalized. Since the organizer selected the sample at random from all trivia teams in the tournament, the largest population to which the results of the study can be generalized is all trivia teams in the tournament.
Choice A is incorrect. The sample was selected at random from all trivia teams in the tournament, not just from the teams that scored an average of $14$ or more points per round.
Choice B is incorrect. If a study uses a sample selected at random from a population, the results of the study can be generalized to the population, not just the sample.
Choice C is incorrect. If a study uses a sample selected at random from a population, the results of the study can be generalized to the population, not just a subset of the sample.

skill: Ratios, rates, proportional relationships, and units
rationale:
Choice A is correct. Since 1 kilometer is equal to 1,000 meters, it follows that 72 kilometers is equal to $72 \times 1 000 = 72 000$ meters. Since 1 hour is equal to 60 minutes and 1 minute is equal to 60 seconds, it follows that 1 hour is equal to $60 \times 60 = 3 600$ seconds. Now the fraction 72 kilometers over 1 hour is equal to the fraction 72,000 meters over 3,600 seconds, which reduces to the fraction 20 meters over 1 second or 20 meters per second.
Choices B, C, and D are incorrect and may result from conceptual or calculation errors.

The best estimate for the total number of trees in the forest is the product of the mean number of trees per acre in the sample and the total number of acres in the forest. This is $(59)(247) = 14,573$, which is between 14,000 and 15,000.
Choice A is incorrect and may result from multiplying the minimum number of trees per acre in the sample, 48, by the number of acres, 247. Choice B is incorrect and may result from multiplying the median number of trees per acre in the sample, 55, by the number of acres, 247. Choice D is incorrect and may result from multiplying the maximum number of trees per acre in the sample, 76, by the number of acres, 247.

rationale: The correct answer is 40. It's given that in Kenya on October 31, 2016, the probability of selecting a Safaricom subscriber from all Internet subscribers is 0.38, that there were p million, or $p \times 1,000,000$, Safaricom subscribers, and that there were 104 million, or 104,000,000, Internet subscribers. The probability of selecting a Safaricom subscriber from all Internet subscribers can be found by dividing the number of Safaricom subscribers by the total number of Internet subscribers. Therefore, the equation $\frac{p \times 1,000,000}{104,000,000} = 0.38$ can be used to solve for p. Dividing 1,000,000 from the numerator and denominator of the expression on the left-hand side yields $\frac{p}{104} = 0.38$. Multiplying both sides of this equation by 104 yields $p = 0.38 \times 104 = 39.52$, which, to the nearest integer, is 40.
text: On June 12, 2016, there were 87 million Internet subscribers in Kenya. The major Internet providers were Safaricom, Airtel, Orange, Faiba, and Telkom. By October 31, 2016, the number of Internet subscribers in Kenya had increased to 104 million. If an Internet subscriber in Kenya on October 31, 2016, is selected at random, the probability that the person selected was a Safaricom subscriber is 0.38. There were p million Safaricom subscribers in Kenya on October 31, 2016. To the nearest integer, what is the value of p?

rationale:
Choice D is correct. The mean amount of time that the 22 employees worked for the company is 6.8 years. This means that the total number of years all 22 employees worked for the company is (6.8)(22) = 149.6 years. After the employee left, the mean amount of time that the remaining 21 employees worked for the company is 6.35 years. Therefore, the total number of years all 21 employees worked for the company is (6.35)(21) = 133.35 years. It follows that the number of years that the employee who left had worked for the company is 149.6 – 133.35 = 16.25 years.
Choice A is incorrect; this is the change in the mean, which isn’t the same as the amount of time worked by the employee who left. Choice B is incorrect and likely results from making the assumption that there were still 22 employees, rather than 21, at the company after the employee left and then subtracting the original mean of 6.8 from that result. Choice C is incorrect and likely results from making the assumption that there were still 22 employees, rather than 21, at the company after the employee left.

It follows that the ratio of the cost per gram of protein in a large egg to the cost per gram of protein in a cup of milk is 0.06:0.03, which can be rewritten as 2:1.
Choice A is incorrect and may result from finding the ratio of the cost per gram of protein in a cup of milk to the cost per gram of protein in a large egg (the reciprocal of the ratio specified in the question). rationale: The correct answer is $\frac{9}{2}$. It's given that the scatterplot shows the relationship between the length of time $y$, in hours, a certain bird spent in flight and the number of days after January $11$, $x$. Since January $13$ is $2$ days after January $11$, it follows that January $13$ corresponds to an x-value of $2$ in the scatterplot. In the scatterplot, when $x=2$, the corresponding value of $y$ is $6$. In other words, on January $13$, the bird spent $6$ hours in flight. Since January $15$ is $4$ days after January $11$, it follows that January $15$ corresponds to an x-value of $4$ in the scatterplot. In the scatterplot, when $x=4$, the corresponding value of $y$ is $15$. In other words, on January $15$, the bird spent $15$ hours in flight. Therefore, the average rate of change, in hours per day, of the length of time the bird spent in flight on January $13$ to the length of time the bird spent in flight on January $15$ is the difference in the length of time, in hours, the bird spent in flight divided by the difference in the number of days after January $11$, or $\frac{15-6}{4-2}$, which is equivalent to $\frac{9}{2}$. Note that 9/2 and 4.5 are examples of ways to enter a correct answer.

rationale:
Choice B is correct. The largest group to which the results of a study can be generalized is the population from which the random sample was chosen. In this case, the psychologist chose a random sample from all students at one particular middle school. Therefore, the largest group to which the results can be generalized is all the students at the school.
Choice A is incorrect because this isn’t the largest group the results can be generalized to. Choices C and D are incorrect because these groups are larger than the population from which the random sample was chosen. Therefore, the sample isn’t representative of these groups.

Choice B is incorrect because 0.07 represents 7 defective wireless earbuds out of 100 rather than out of 1,000. rationale:
Choice B is correct. It's given that the value of $z$ is $1.18$ times $100$. This can be written as $z= \left(1.18\right) \left(100\right)$, which is equivalent to $z= \left(1+0.18\right)(100)$, or $z= \left(1+\frac{18}{100}\right) \left(100\right)$. It follows that the value of $z$ is $100%$ of $100$ plus $18%$ of $100$. Therefore, the value of $z$ is $18%$ greater than $100$.
Choice A is incorrect. This gives a value of $z$ that is $1.118$, not $1.18$, times $100$.
Choice C is incorrect. This gives a value of $z$ that is $2.80$, not $1.18$, times $100$.
Choice D is incorrect. This gives a value of $z$ that is $3.18$, not $1.18$, times $100$.
text: The value of $z$ is $1.18$ times $100$. The value of $z$ is what percent greater than $100$?

skill: Evaluating statistical claims: Observational studies and experiments
rationale:
Choice A is correct. Statement I need not be true. The fact that 64% of the 850 adults who were surveyed responded that they were satisfied with the reliability of public transportation in the city does not mean that the exact same percentage of <u>all</u> adults in the city will be satisfied with the reliability of public transportation in the city. Statement II need not be true because random samples, even when they are of the same size, are not necessarily identical with regard to percentages of people in them who have a certain opinion. Statement III need not be true for the same reason that statement II need not be true: results from different samples can vary. The variation may be even bigger for this sample since it would be selected from a different city. Therefore, none of the statements must be true.
Choices B, C, and D are incorrect because none of the statements must be true.

category.subject: Math
category.skill: Evaluating statistical claims: Observational studies and experiments

text:
A polling agency recently surveyed 850 adults who were selected at random from a large city and asked each of the adults, "Are you satisfied with the reliability of public transportation in the city?" Of those surveyed, 64 percent responded that they were satisfied with the reliability of public transportation in the city. Based on the results of the survey, which of the following statements must be true?
I. Of all adults in the city, 64 percent are satisfied with the reliability of public transportation in the city.
II. If another 850 adults selected at random from the city were surveyed, 64 percent of them would report they are satisfied with the reliability of public transportation in the city.
III. If 850 adults selected at random from a different city were surveyed, 64 percent of them would report they are satisfied with the reliability of public transportation in the city.

rationale:
Choice C is correct. It's given that an estimated 41% of sampled students at the school were in support of a schedule change, with a margin of error of 6%. It follows that the percent of the students at the school who support a schedule change is 41% plus or minus 6%. The lower bound of this estimation is $41 - 6$, or 35%. The upper bound of this estimation is $41 + 6$, or 47%. Therefore, plausible values of the percent of the students at the school who support a schedule change are between 35% and 47%.
Choice A is incorrect. This is the percent of the sampled students at the school who support a schedule change. Choices B and D are incorrect and may result from misinterpreting the margin of error.

rationale:
Choice B is correct. There were 64 days with no rain. It was a weekend day for 16 of those 64 days. So 16 out of 64 of the days with no rain were weekend days. Because the day is selected at random, each day has an equal chance of being selected, so the probability is $\frac{16}{64} = \frac{1}{4}$.
Choice A is incorrect. It is the probability that a day selected at random from any one of the days during the 12 weeks is a weekend day with no rain. Choice C is incorrect. It is the probability that a day selected at random from the weekend days has no rain. Choice D is incorrect. It is the probability that a day selected at random from the days with no rain is a weekday.

rationale:
Choice C is correct. Let $x$ be the 2017 population of Brookhaven. It's given that the population increased by $9\%$ from 2017 to 2018. The increase in population can be written as $\left(0.09\right)x$. The 2018 population of Brookhaven is given as the sum of the 2017 population of Brookhaven and the increase in population from 2017 to 2018. This can be rewritten as $x+ \left(0.09\right)x$, or $1.09x$. Therefore, the value of $k$ is $1.09$.
Choice A is incorrect. This is the percent, represented as a decimal, that the population increased from 2017 to 2018, not the value of $k$.
Choice B is incorrect and may result from conceptual or calculation errors.
Choice D is incorrect. This is the value of $k$ if the population increased by $90\%$, not $9\%$, from 2017 to 2018.

rationale: Choice D is correct. A linear model can be written in the form $y=mx+b$, where $m$ is the slope of the graph of the model in the xy-plane and $(0,b)$ is the y-intercept. The graph of an appropriate linear model for this relationship passes near the points $(1,3)$ and $(9,10)$ in the xy-plane. Two points on a line, $\left({x}_{1},{y}_{1}\right)$ and $\left({x}_{2},{y}_{2}\right)$, can be used to find the slope of the line using the slope formula, $m=\frac{{y}_{2}-{y}_{1}}{{x}_{2}-{x}_{1}}$. Substituting the points $(1,3)$ and $(9,10)$ for $\left({x}_{1},{y}_{1}\right)$ and $\left({x}_{2},{y}_{2}\right)$, respectively, in the slope formula yields $m=\frac{10-3}{9-1}$, or $m=0.875$. Therefore, the value of $m$ for an appropriate linear model is approximately $0.875$. Substituting $0.875$ for $m$ in $y=mx+b$ yields $y=0.875x+b$. Since an appropriate linear model passes near the point $(1,3)$, the approximate value of $b$ can be found by substituting $1$ for $x$ and $3$ for $y$ in the equation $y=0.875x+b$, which yields $3=(0.875)(1)+b$, or $3=0.875+b$. Subtracting $0.875$ from both sides of this equation yields $2.125=b$. Therefore, the value of $b$ for an appropriate linear model is approximately $2.125$. Thus, of the given choices, $y=0.9x+2.2$ is the most appropriate linear model for this relationship. Alternate approach: A linear model can be written in the form $y=mx+b$, where $m$ is the slope of the graph of the model in the xy-plane and $(0,b)$ is the y-intercept. The scatterplot shows that as the x-values of the data points increase, the y-values of the data points increase, which means the graph of an appropriate linear model has a positive slope. Of the given choices, $y=0.9x+2.2$ is the only linear model whose graph has a positive slope. Choice A is incorrect. The graph of this model has a negative slope, not a positive slope. Choice B is incorrect. The graph of this model has a negative slope, not a positive slope. Choice C is incorrect. The graph of this model has a negative slope, not a positive slope.

The scatterplot shows the relationship between two variables, $x$ and $y$.
Which equation is the most appropriate linear model for this relationship?

skill: Ratios, rates, proportional relationships, and units
rationale:
Choice C is correct. Since $1$ meter is equal to $100$ centimeters, $64$ meters is equal to $64\text{meters} \left(\frac{100\text{centimeters}}{1\text{meter}}\right)$, or $6,400$ centimeters.
Choice A is incorrect and may result from conceptual or calculation errors.
Choice B is incorrect and may result from dividing, rather than multiplying, $64$ by $100$.
Choice D is incorrect. This is the length, in millimeters rather than centimeters, that is equivalent to a length of $64$ meters.

rationale: The correct answer is $\frac{23}{60}$. It's given that one of the participants will be selected at random. The probability of selecting a participant from group A given that the participant is at least $10$ years of age is the number of participants in group A who are at least $10$ years of age divided by the total number of participants who are at least $10$ years of age. The table shows that in group A, there are $14$ participants who are $10$–$19$ years of age and $9$ participants who are $20+$ years of age. Therefore, there are $14+9$, or $23$, participants in group A who are at least $10$ years of age. The table also shows that there are a total of $30$ participants who are $10$–$19$ years of age and $30$ participants who are $20+$ years of age. Therefore, there are a total of $30+30$, or $60$, participants who are at least $10$ years of age. It follows that the probability of selecting a participant from group A given that the participant is at least $10$ years of age is $\frac{23}{60}$. Note that 23/60,.3833, and 0.383 are examples of ways to enter a correct answer.
text: The table summarizes the distribution of age and assigned group for $90$ participants in a study. One of these participants will be selected at random. What is the probability of selecting a participant from group A, given that the participant is at least $10$ years of age? (Express your answer as a decimal or fraction, not as a percent.)

rationale:
Choice B is correct. It's given that in a collection, $50%$ of the items are blue. It follows that the number of blue items in the collection can be represented by $0.5x$, where $x$ represents the total number of items in the collection. It's also given that of all the blue items in the collection, $24%$ also have labels. It follows that the number of items in the collection that are blue and have labels can be represented by $0.24 \left(0.5x\right)$, or $0.12x$. The expression $0.12x$ represents $12%$ of $x$. Since $x$ represents the total number of items in the collection, it follows that $12%$ of the items in the collection are blue and have labels.
Choice A is incorrect and may result from subtracting $24%$ from $50%$ rather than calculating $24%$ of $50%$.
Choice C is incorrect and may result from adding $24%$ and $50%$ rather than calculating $24%$ of $50%$.
Choice D is incorrect and may result from calculating the percentage that $24%$ is of $50%$ rather than calculating $24%$ of $50%$.
text: In a collection, $50\%$ of the items are blue. Of all the blue items in the collection, $24\%$ also have labels. What percentage of the items in the collection are blue with labels?

rationale: The correct answer is $370.5$. It's given that the number $b$ is $280%$ greater than $150$. It follows that $b$ is equal to $150$ plus $280%$ of $150$, which can be written as $b=150+2.8(150)$, or $b=570$. It's also given that the number $a$ is $35%$ less than the number $b$. It follows that $a$ is equal to $b$ minus $35%$ of $b$, which can be written as $a=b-0.35b$, or $a=0.65b$. Substituting $570$ for $b$ in this equation yields $a=0.65(570)$, or $a=370.5$.
text: The number $a$ is $35\%$ less than the number $b$. The number $b$ is $280\%$ greater than $150$. What is the value of $a$?

skill: Ratios, rates, proportional relationships, and units
rationale:
Choice D is correct. Since the number of yards in $1$ mile is $1,760$, the number of square yards in $1$ square mile is $(1,760)(1,760)=3,097,600$. Therefore, if the area of the township is $5.12$ square miles, it is $5.12(3,097,600)=15,859,712$, in square yards.
Choice A is incorrect and may result from dividing the number of yards in a mile by the square mileage of the township.
Choice B is incorrect and may result from multiplying the number of yards in a mile by the square mileage of the township.
Choice C is incorrect and may result from dividing the number of square yards in a square mile by the square mileage of the township.

rationale:
Choice B is correct. Range is defined as the difference between the greatest and least values from a set of data. The greatest number of wheels made during a one-hour period was 24 wheels. The least number of wheels was 19. Hence, the range is $24 - 19 = 5$, or 5.0.
Choices A, C, and D are incorrect and may be the result of arithmetic errors or incorrectly identifying the greatest or least number of wheels made during a one-hour period.

rationale: Choice A is correct. An equation of a line of best fit can be written in the form $y = a + b x$, where a is the y-intercept of the line and b is the slope. In the scatterplot shown, the line of best fit intersects the y-axis just over halfway between 10,000 and 20,000, or approximately 16,000. The line of best fit also intersects the graph at the point with coordinates $(5, 40,000)$. Using the slope formula $b = \frac{y_2 - y_1}{x_2 - x_1}$ and two points that lie on the graph such as the point with coordinates $(5, 40,000)$ and the point with coordinates $(0, 16,000)$, the slope can be approximated as $\frac{40,000 - 16,000}{5 - 0} = 4,800$. Only choice A has a y-intercept near the estimate of 16,000 and a slope near the estimate of 4,800. Therefore, an equation of the line of best fit could be $n = 16,090 + 4,680t$. Choice B is incorrect because the values for the slope and the y-coordinate of the y-intercept are switched. Choice C is incorrect because the value for the slope is approximately double the actual slope. Choice D is incorrect because the values for the slope and the y-intercept are switched and because the slope is approximately double the actual slope.

rationale: The correct answer is $71,280$. It's given that $1\text{furlong}=220\text{yards}$ and $1\text{yard}=3\text{feet}$. It follows that a distance of $108$ furlongs is equivalent to $\left(108\text{furlongs}\right) \left(\frac{220\text{yards}}{1\text{furlong}}\right) \left(\frac{3\text{feet}}{1\text{yard}}\right)$, or $71,280$ feet.

rationale: Choice C is correct. The decimal equivalent of 8% is 0.08. Since increasing the number n by 8% yields the number 432, this situation can be represented by the equation $n \times (1 + 0.08) = 432$, or $n \times 1.08 = 432$. Dividing both sides of this equation by 1.08 yields $n = 400$. Choice A is incorrect. This is the result when n is increased by 80%, not by 8%. Choice B is incorrect. This is the approximate result of decreasing 432 by 8%. Choice D is incorrect. This is the approximate result of increasing 432 by 8%.
text: A number n is increased 8%. If the result is 432, what is the value of n?

rationale:
Choice A is correct. It's given that the result of increasing the quantity $x$ by $300%$ is $84$. This can be written as $x+ \left(\frac{300}{100}\right)x=84$, which is equivalent to $x+3x=84$, or $4x=84$. Dividing each side of this equation by $4$ yields $x=21$. Therefore, the value of $x$ is $21$.
Choice B is incorrect. The result of increasing the quantity $28$ by $300%$ is $112$, not $84$.
Choice C is incorrect. The result of increasing the quantity $252$ by $300%$ is $1,008$, not $84$.
Choice D is incorrect. The result of increasing the quantity $216$ by $300%$ is $864$, not $84$.
text: The result of increasing the quantity $x$ by $300\%$ is $84$. What is the value of $x$?

rationale: Choice C is correct. On the line of best fit, an x-value of $1,200$ corresponds to a y-value between $10$ and $12$. Therefore, of the given choices, $11$ is closest to the y-value predicted by the line of best fit at $x=1,200$. Choice A is incorrect. This is the integer value closest to the y-value predicted by the line of best fit at $x=1,800$. Choice B is incorrect. This is the integer value closest to the y-value predicted by the line of best fit at $x=1,500$. Choice D is incorrect. This is the integer value closest to the y-value predicted by the line of best fit at $x=600$.
text: Twelve data points are shown in the scatterplot. A line of best fit for the data is also shown. At $x=1,200$, which of the following is closest to the y-value predicted by the line of best fit?

skill: Evaluating statistical claims: Observational studies and experiments
rationale:
Choice D is correct. The sample was selected from a group of people who indicated that they liked the novel. It is inappropriate to generalize the result of the survey beyond the population from which the participants were selected. Choice D is the most appropriate inference from the survey results because it describes a conclusion about people who liked the novel, and the results of the survey indicate that most people who like the novel disliked the show.
Choices A, B, and C are incorrect because none of these inferences can be drawn from the survey results. Choices A and B need not be true. The people surveyed all liked the novel on which the show was based, which is not necessarily true of all people who watch television shows or all people who read novels. Thus, the people surveyed are not representative of all people who watch television shows or all people who read novels. Therefore, the results of this survey cannot appropriately be extended to at least 92% of people who watch television shows or to at least 92% of people who read novels. Choice C need not be true because the sample includes only people who liked the novel, and so the results do not extend to people who dislike the novel.

category.subject: Math

text: A market researcher selected 180 people at random from a group of people who indicated that they liked a certain novel. The 180 people were shown a television show based on the novel and then asked whether they liked or disliked the show. Of those surveyed, 92% said they disliked the show. Which of the following inferences can appropriately be drawn from this survey result?

choices.text: At least 92% of people who watch television shows will dislike this show.
At least 92% of people who read novels will dislike this show.
Most people who dislike this novel will like this show.
Most people who like this novel will dislike this show.

rationale:
Choice B is correct. The median weight is found by ordering the horses’ weights from least to greatest and then determining the middle value from this list of weights. Decreasing the value for the horse with the lowest weight doesn’t affect the median since it’s still the lowest value.
Choice A is incorrect. The mean is calculated by finding the sum of all the weights of the horses and then dividing by the number of horses. Decreasing one of the weights would decrease the sum and therefore decrease the mean. Choice C is incorrect. Range is the difference between the highest and lowest weights, so decreasing the lowest weight would increase the range. Choice D is incorrect. Standard deviation is calculated based on the mean weight of the horses. Decreasing one of the weights decreases the mean and therefore would affect the standard deviation.

Which value remains the same if the four values are reported using the corrected weight?

rationale: Choice A is correct. The line of best fit shown has a positive slope and intersects the y-axis at a positive y-value. The graph of an equation of the form $y=mx+b$, where $m$ and $b$ are constants, has a slope of $m$ and intersects the y-axis at a y-value of $b$. Of the given choices, only $y=x+3.4$ represents a line that has a positive slope, $1$, and intersects the y-axis at a positive y-value, $3.4$. Choice B is incorrect. This equation represents a line that intersects the y-axis at a negative y-value, not a positive y-value. Choice C is incorrect. This equation represents a line that has a negative slope, not a positive slope. Choice D is incorrect. This equation represents a line that has a negative slope, not a positive slope, and intersects the y-axis at a negative y-value, not a positive y-value.

rationale:
Choice A is correct. It's given that set K consists of all the positive integers that are less than or equal to $180$, where none of the integers are repeated. Therefore, set K consists of the integers $1$ through $180$, which is $180$ numbers. Of these, the integers $1$ through $54$ are less than or equal to $54$. Therefore, there are $54$ numbers that belong to set K that are less than or equal to $54$. Of these, half are even. Therefore, $\frac{54}{2}$, or $27$, numbers that belong to set K are even and less than or equal to $54$. The probability of selecting at random a number from set K that is even and less than or equal to $54$ is equal to the number of numbers that belong to set K that are even and less than or equal to $54$, which is $27$, divided by the total number of numbers that belong to set K, which is $180$. It follows that the probability of selecting at random from set K a number that is even and less than or equal to $54$ is $\frac{27}{180}$, or $\frac{3}{20}$.
Choice B is incorrect and may result from conceptual or calculation errors.
Choice C is incorrect and may result from dividing the number of favorable outcomes by the number of integers that are even or less than or equal to $54$.
Choice D is incorrect. This is the probability of selecting at random a number from set K that is less than or equal to $54$, not a number that is even and less than or equal to $54$.
text: Set K consists of all the positive integers that are less than or equal to $180$, where none of the integers are repeated. If a number is selected at random from set K, what is the probability of selecting a number that is even and less than or equal to $54$?

rationale: The correct answer is $19$. If one value is multiplied by a number, then the other value must be multiplied by the same number in order to maintain the same ratio. It's given that $m$ is multiplied by $19$. Therefore, in order to maintain the same ratio, $n$ must also be multiplied by $19$.

rationale: The correct answer is $91$. It's given that there are a total of $325$ participants and each participant is assigned to either group A, group B, or group C. It's also given that if one of these participants is selected at random, the probability of selecting a participant who is assigned to group A is $0.48$ and the probability of selecting a participant who is assigned to group B is $0.24$. It follows that there are $0.48 \left(325\right)$, or $156$, participants who are assigned to group A and $0.24 \left(325\right)$, or $78$, participants who are assigned to group B. The number of participants who are assigned to group C is the number of participants who are not assigned to group A or group B. In other words, the number of participants who are assigned to group C is the total number of participants minus the number of participants who are assigned to group A and group B. Therefore, the number of participants who are assigned to group C is $325-156-78$, or $91$.
text: At a workshop, there are a total of $325$ participants. Each participant is assigned to either group A, group B, or group C. If one of these participants is selected at random, the probability of selecting a participant who is assigned to group A is $0.48$ and the probability of selecting a participant who is assigned to group B is $0.24$. How many participants are assigned to group C?

rationale:
Choice B is correct. It′s given that the weather balloon descends at a constant rate of $350\text{feet}\text{per}\text{minute}$. Since the altitude decreases by a constant amount during each fixed time period, the relationship between the weather balloon′s altitude and time is linear. Since the weather balloon descends from an altitude of $8,800\text{feet}$ to $4,600\text{feet}$, the weather balloon′s altitude is decreasing with time. Thus, the relationship is best modeled by a decreasing linear function.
Choice A is incorrect and may result from conceptual or calculation errors.
Choice C is incorrect and may result from conceptual or calculation errors.
Choice D is incorrect and may result from conceptual or calculation errors.
text: A weather balloon descends from an altitude of $8,800$ feet to $4,600$ feet at a constant rate of $350$ feet per minute. What type of function best models the relationship between the descending weather balloon's altitude and time?

skill: Ratios, rates, proportional relationships, and units
rationale:
Choice A is correct. At the exchange rate of 1 peso = 0.065 US dollars, 8,000 pesos would be converted to 8,000 × 0.065 = \$520.00. However, since Lina pays a 3% fee on the converted US dollar amount, she receives only (100 – 3)%, or 97%, of the converted US dollars, and 520.00 × 0.97 = \$504.40.
Choice B is incorrect. This is the number of US dollars Lina would receive if the exchange service did not charge a 3% fee. Choice C is incorrect and may result from a decimal point error made when calculating the conversion to US dollars and from not assessing the 3% fee. Choice D is incorrect and may result from reversing the units of the exchange rate.

text: Lina has 8,000 pesos she will convert to US dollars using a currency exchange service. At this time, the currency exchange rate is 1 peso = 0.065 US dollars. The exchange service will charge Lina a 3% fee on the converted US dollar amount. How many US dollars will Lina receive from the currency exchange after the 3% fee is applied?

rationale:
Choice B is correct. The median of a data set with an odd number of data values is defined as the middle value of the ordered list of values. The data set shown has nine values, so the median is the fifth value in the ordered list, which is $82$.
Choice A is incorrect. This is the minimum value of the data set, not the median.
Choice C is incorrect and may result from averaging the median and the next value, $\frac{82+86}{2}=84$, instead of identifying the middle value.
Choice D is incorrect. This is the next data value after the median, not the median.

skill: Ratios, rates, proportional relationships, and units
rationale: The correct answer is $.30$. The volume of a cube is given by the formula $V={s}^{3}$, where $s$ is the length of an edge of the cube. It's given that each edge of the cube has a length of $3.00$ centimeters. Substituting $3.00$ centimeters for $s$ in the formula $V={s}^{3}$ yields $V=(3.00\text{centimeters})^{3}$, or $V=27.00$ cubic centimeters. It's given that the cube has a mass of $8.10$ grams. Dividing the mass, in grams, of the cube by the volume, in cubic centimeters, of the cube gives its density, in grams per cubic centimeters. Therefore, the density of the cube is $\frac{8.10\text{grams}}{27.00\text{cubic}\text{centimeters}}$, or $.30$ grams per cubic centimeter. Note that .30 and 3/10 are examples of ways to enter a correct answer.

The length of the edge of one of these cubes is $3.00$ centimeters. If this cube has a mass of $8.10$ grams, what is the density of this cube, in grams per cubic centimeter?

rationale: The correct answer is 285. The number of people predicted to visit the beach each day is represented by the y-values of the line of best fit, and the average temperature, in degrees Celsius (degrees Celsius), is represented by the x-values. Since the slope of the line of best fit is approximately 57, the y-value, or the number of people predicted to visit the beach each day, increases by 57 for every x-value increase of 1, or every 1 degree Celsius increase in average temperature. Therefore, an increase of 5 degrees Celsius in average temperature corresponds to a y-value increase of $57 \times 5 = 285$ additional people per day predicted to visit the beach.
text: Each dot in the scatterplot above represents the temperature and the number of people who visited a beach in Lagos, Nigeria, on one of eleven different days. The line of best fit for the data is also shown. The line of best fit for the data has a slope of approximately 57. According to this estimate, how many additional people per day are predicted to visit the beach for each 5°C increase in average temperature?

rationale:
Choice A is correct. The mean of a data set is the sum of the values divided by the number of values. The mean of data set A is $\frac{54}{9}$, or $6$. The mean of data set B is $\frac{144}{10}$, or $14.4$. Thus, the means are different. The median of a data set is the middle value when the values are ordered from least to greatest. The medians of data sets A and B are both 6. Therefore, the medians are the same, so only the means are different.
Choices B, C, and D are incorrect and may result from conceptual or calculation errors.

Neither the means nor the medians are different.

skill: Ratios, rates, proportional relationships, and units
rationale: The correct answer is $178.4$. It's given that an area of $52.00$ square nautical miles is equivalent to $k$ square kilometers. Since $1$ nautical mile is equal to $1.852$ kilometers, it follows that an area of $1$ square nautical mile is equivalent to ${(1.852)}^{2}$, or $3.429904$, square kilometers. Multiplying $52.00$ by $3.429904$ yields $178.355008$ square kilometers. Rounding this value to the nearest tenth yields $178.4$. Therefore, the value of $k$ is $178.4$.

rationale: Choice A is correct. A line of best fit for the data in a scatterplot is a line that follows the trend of the data with approximately half the data points above and half the data points below the line. Based on the given data, a line of best fit will have a positive y-intercept on or near the point with coordinates 0 comma 6 and a negative slope. All of the choices are in slope-intercept form $y = m x + b$, where m is the slope and b is the y-coordinate of the y-intercept. Only choice A is an equation of a line with a positive y-intercept at the point with coordinates 0 comma 6 and a negative slope, $- 1$. Choice B is incorrect. This equation is for a line that has a negative y-intercept, not a positive y-intercept. Choices C and D are incorrect and may result from one or more sign errors and from switching the values of the y-intercept and the slope in the equation.

rationale:
Choice C is correct. The table gives the results of $200$ people who were asked how often they see a movie in a theater. The table shows that $29$ people responded "never" and $53$ people responded "almost never." Therefore, $29+53$, or $82$, people responded either "never" or "almost never."
Choice A is incorrect. This is the difference between the number of people who responded "almost never" and the number of people who responded "never."
Choice B is incorrect. This is the number of people who responded "almost never" but doesn't include those who responded "never."
Choice D is incorrect. This is the number of people who responded something other than "never" or "almost never," rather than the number of people who responded either "never" or "almost never."

rationale:
Choice D is correct. To find the percent increase of the customer's gas bill, the absolute increase of the bill, in dollars, is divided by the original amount of the bill, and the result is multiplied by 100%, as follows: $\frac{86.12 - 81.25}{81.25} \approx 0.060$; $0.060 \times 100\% = 6.0\%$.
Choice A is incorrect. This choice is the difference $86.12 - 81.25$ rounded to the nearest tenth, which is the (absolute) increase of the bill's amount, not its percent increase. Choice B is incorrect and may be the result of some calculation errors. rationale:
Choice C is correct. The median of a data set with an odd number of values, in ascending or descending order, is the middle value of the data set, and the range of a data set is the positive difference between the maximum and minimum values in the data set. Since the dot plot shown gives the values in data set A in ascending order and there are $15$ values in the data set, the eighth value in data set A, $23$, is the median. The maximum value in data set A is $26$ and the minimum value is $22$, so the range of data set A is $26-22$, or $4$. It's given that data set B is created by adding $56$ to each of the values in data set A. Increasing each of the $15$ values in data set A by $56$ will also increase its median value by $56$ making the median of data set B $79$. Increasing each value of data set A by $56$ does not change the range, since the maximum value of data set B is $26+56$, or $82$, and the minimum value is $22+56$, or $78$, making the range of data set B $82-78$, or $4$. Therefore, the median of data set B is greater than the median of data set A, and the range of data set B is equal to the range of data set A.
Choice A is incorrect and may result from conceptual or calculation errors.
Choice B is incorrect and may result from conceptual or calculation errors.

rationale: The correct answer is $.49$. It's given that the number $a$ is $65%$ less than the positive number $b$. Therefore, $c= \left(1+\frac{40}{100}\right)a$, which is equivalent to $c= \left(1+0.40\right)a$, or $c=1.40a$. Since $a=0.35b$, substituting $0.35b$ for $a$ in the equation $c=1.40a$ yields $c=1.40 \left(0.35b\right)$, or $c=0.49b$. Thus, $c$ is $0.49$ times $b$. Note that.49 and 49/100 are examples of ways to enter a correct answer.

rationale:

In this case, the people who responded were people with access to streaming news and websites, which aren’t accessible to the entire population. Moreover, the people who responded also chose to watch the program and respond to the poll. The people who made these choices aren’t representative of the entire population of the United States because they were not a random sample of the population of the United States.
Choices A, C, and D are incorrect because they present reasons unrelated to whether the sample is representative of the population of the United States.

skill: Ratios, rates, proportional relationships, and units
rationale:
Choice B is correct. It's given that object M travels a distance of $5x$ inches in $y$ seconds. This speed can be written as $\frac{5x\text{inches}}{y\text{seconds}}$. It's given that the speed of object M is half the speed of object N. It follows that the speed of object N is twice the speed of object M, which is $2 \left(\frac{5x\text{inches}}{y\text{seconds}}\right)$, or $\frac{10x\text{inches}}{y\text{seconds}}$. Let $n$ represent the time, in seconds, it takes object N to travel a distance of $40x$ inches. The value of $n$ can be found by solving the equation $\frac{10x\text{inches}}{y\text{seconds}}=\frac{40x\text{inches}}{n\text{seconds}}$, which can be written as $\frac{10x}{y}=\frac{40x}{n}$. Multiplying each side of this equation by $ny$ yields $10xn=40xy$. Dividing each side of this equation by $10x$ yields $n=4y$. Therefore, the expression $4y$ represents the time, in seconds, it takes object N to travel a distance of $40x$ inches.
Choice A is incorrect. This expression represents the time, in seconds, it would take object N to travel a distance of $40x$ inches if the speed of object M were twice, not half, the speed of object N.
Choice C is incorrect. This expression represents the time, in seconds, it takes object N to travel a distance of $300x$ inches, not $40x$ inches.
Choice D is incorrect. This expression represents the time, in seconds, it takes object M, not object N, to travel a distance of $40x$ inches.

skill: One-variable data: Distributions and measures of center and spread
rationale: The correct answer is $46$. The mean of a data set is computed by dividing the sum of the values in the data set by the number of values in the data set. It's given that data set A consists of the heights of $72$ objects and has a mean of $30$ meters. This can be represented by the equation $\frac{x}{72}=30$, where $x$ represents the sum of the heights of the objects, in meters, in data set A. Multiplying both sides of this equation by $72$ yields $x=72 \left(30\right)$, or $x=2,160$ meters. Therefore, the sum of the heights of the objects in data set A is $2,160$ meters. It's also given that data set B consists of the heights of $48$ objects and has a mean of $70$ meters. This can be represented by the equation $\frac{y}{48}=70$, where $y$ represents the sum of the heights of the objects, in meters, in data set B. Multiplying both sides of this equation by $48$ yields $y=48 \left(70\right)$, or $y=3,360$ meters. Therefore, the sum of the heights of the objects in data set B is $3,360$ meters. Since it's given that data set C consists of the heights of the $120$ objects from data sets A and B, it follows that the mean of data set C is the sum of the heights of the objects, in meters, in data sets A and B divided by the number of objects represented in data sets A and B, or $\frac{2,160+3,360}{120}$, which is equivalent to $46$ meters. Therefore, the mean, in meters, of data set C is $46$.

Since the integer $12$ is less than all the integers in data set F, the mean of data set G must be less than the mean of data set F. Thus, the mean of data set F isn't less than the mean of data set G. When a data set is in ascending order, the median is between the two middle values when there is an even number of values and the median is the middle value when there is an odd number of values. It follows that the median of data set F is either greater than or equal to the median of data set G. Therefore, the median of data set F isn't less than the median of data set G. Thus, neither the mean nor the median must be less for data set F than for data set G.
Choice A is incorrect and may result from conceptual or calculation errors.

skill: Inference from sample statistics and margin of error
rationale:
Choice A is correct. It's given that based on a random sample from a population, the estimated mean value for a certain variable for the population is $23.4$, with an associated margin of error of $1.3$. This means that it is plausible that the actual mean value of the variable for the population is between $23.4-1.3$ and $23.4+1.3$. Therefore, the most appropriate conclusion is that it is plausible that the actual mean value of the variable for the population is between $22.1$ and $24.7$.
Choice B is incorrect. The estimated mean value and associated margin of error describe only plausible values, not all the possible values, for the actual mean value of the variable, so this is not an appropriate conclusion.
Choice C is incorrect. The estimated mean value and associated margin of error describe only plausible values for the actual mean value of the variable, not all the possible values of the variable, so this is not an appropriate conclusion.
Choice D is incorrect. Since $23.4$ is the estimated mean value of the variable based on a random sample, the actual mean value of the variable may not be exactly $23.4$. Therefore, this is not an appropriate conclusion.
